top_stories.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
top_stories.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign enabled credential theft without deploying malware by targeting router flaws to silently siphon authentication tokens at scale.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the security challenges posed by AI agents as highly privileged identities that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are inadequate to manage. The piece focuses on the concept of eliminating standing privilege in favor of just-in-time access models to address risks introduced by AI agent identities.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is conducting a corporate breach campaign by impersonating support staff and using fake Okta login pages to steal credentials and gain access to corporate systems. The campaign combines social engineering with phishing infrastructure mimicking Okta to exfiltrate sensitive data from targeted organizations.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws, making this patch cycle particularly relevant to access control and privileged access management.

top_stories.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the need for cybersecurity teams to shift their detection models to identify breaches that mimic normal business activity. The focus is on detecting identity-based intrusions where attackers use legitimate credentials, making it directly relevant to IAM and authentication security practices.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russian APT group Fancy Bear is continuing its global cyberattack campaign targeting organizations across multiple sectors. Security experts recommend patching and zero trust implementation as non-negotiable defensive measures against the threat.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops outlines five ways Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on limiting access and preventing privilege escalation from stolen credentials. The piece covers identity-first Zero Trust principles including device trust enforcement and lateral movement prevention.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials in fraud attempts exceeding $20 million. The W3LL toolkit was a commercially available phishing-as-a-service platform specifically designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature designed to block session theft by binding browser sessions to the device. DBSC aims to prevent cookie hijacking attacks where stolen session tokens are used to bypass authentication, with macOS support planned for a future Chrome release.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real-time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites rather than using static copied pages. The service effectively bypasses MFA protections by acting as a man-in-the-middle between the victim and the authentic website, making traditional phishing defenses less effective.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: A new open-source tool called Prmana replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the Linux host through PAM, using DPoP (RFC 9449) for cryptographic proof of token possession. The tool is written in Rust, licensed under Apache 2.0, and integrates OIDC-based authentication directly into the Linux PAM stack for SSH access.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge in a Help Net Security interview. The conversation draws on his experience at IBM and CyberArk to address visibility and governance approaches for non-human identities.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-based keys to cryptographically bind session cookies to specific devices on Windows. This directly counters infostealer malware that steals session cookies to bypass authentication, addressing a common method used to circumvent MFA and session-based access controls.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks where infostealer malware extracts and exfiltrates authentication cookies. The feature cryptographically binds session credentials to a specific device, preventing stolen cookies from being used on attacker-controlled systems.

news.title: Google Chrome 146 Adds Device Bound Session Credentials to Stop Session Cookie Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has introduced Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature designed to prevent session cookie theft. DBSC cryptographically binds browser sessions to a specific device, making stolen session cookies unusable on other devices and directly addressing a common authentication bypass technique.

news.title: Google Chrome adds infostealer protection against session cookie theft
news.summary: Google has rolled out Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature that cryptographically binds session cookies to a specific device to prevent theft by infostealer malware. DBSC uses public-key cryptography tied to the device's hardware, meaning stolen session cookies cannot be replayed by attackers on different machines.

news.title: 'Several dozen' high-value corporations hit by new extortion crew in helpdesk phishing spree
news.summary: A new extortion group has compromised several dozen high-value corporations by exploiting helpdesk social engineering and phishing attacks, according to Google. The campaign targets identity verification weaknesses at IT helpdesks to gain unauthorized access, a technique closely associated with vishing/MFA bypass attacks used by threat actors like Scattered Spider.

news.title: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
news.summary: Microsoft's DART team has identified a financially motivated threat actor, Storm-2755, targeting Canadian employee accounts to gain unauthorized access and redirect payroll. The attacks focus on compromising employee identity and account access to divert salary payments, making it directly relevant to authentication and account security practices.
